stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Ending war before winter ‘correct and realistic’, says senior Kyiv aide
stories.summary: Budanov backs Zelenskyy call to capitalise on Kyiv’s strong position on battlefield; Kyiv under heavy attack into Tuesday morning. What we know on day 1,560 A deal to end the war against Russia by winter is a “realistic” outcome , Kyrylo Budanov said. The celebrated former Ukrainian spymaster, now Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s chief of staff, said on Monday: “This is the president’s instruction: to try to end this war as soon as possible … preferably before winter. In my opinion, this is absolutely corr

stories.title: ‘Extremist’ Israeli ministers face EU sanctions push
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries will be asked to support sanctions on Israeli politicians accused of breaching the rights of foreign activists detained as part of a flotilla that attempted to reach Gaza. Capitals are set to consider imposing the restrictions on senior members of Israel’s government for the first time ahead of a meeting of leaders at the European Council on June 18-19, according to a draft document worked up by officials and obtained by POLITICO . The proposals will be discussed by ambas

stories.title: Cyclist praised for rescuing four people who nearly drowned in Ruhr, Germany
stories.summary: Family were enjoying a waterside barbecue in Bochum before members ended up in jeopardy in river A cyclist has been praised for having “stepped in decisively” and rescuing four members of a family who nearly drowned in the Ruhr River in Germany during a waterside barbecue that almost ended in tragedy. The family of eight had set themselves up on the riverbank in the western city of Bochum, the local fire brigade reported, but the gathering took a panicked turn when one woman got too close to the

stories.title: Hungarian PM threatens to oust Orbán-era president
stories.summary: Hungary's president has refused Prime Minister Péter Magyar's demands to step aside, setting up a constitutional clash.

stories.title: Lithuania turns pink for giant cold soup festival
stories.summary: More than 100,000 people are expected to attend Vilnius’s Pink Soup Festival, a three-day celebration of Lithuania’s famous cold beet soup featuring races, costumes, foam slides and mass communal lunches.

stories.title: Hungary's PM Peter Magyar moves to oust president
stories.summary: Hungary's new Prime Minister, Peter Magyar, has moved to oust the president, threatening constitutional action. Head of state Tamas Sulyok has refused Magyar's demand that he resign.

stories.title: Poland's controversial 'Highway to Hel' 666 bus revived
stories.summary: Poland's seaside resort called Hel is getting its 666 bus service back, despite earlier opposition from Christian groups.

stories.title: 20 millió forintnyi büntetést kapott egy teniszező a Roland Garroson, mert azt mondta, nem kéne a nőknek bírónak lenni

stories.summary: A Karmelita és a minisztériumi túrák után a Nemzeti Bank székházában videózott a miniszterelnök.

stories.summary: Borbély vezetése alatt a Győri ETO idén megnyerte a magyar focibajnokságot.

stories.summary: Hiába mondta be a pilóta, hogy ez nagyon nem vicces, és a protokoll szerint a hatóságoknak biztonsági ellenőrzést kell tartani.

stories.title: Egyszer csak a fejemen landolt egy kerti bútor – ilyen volt Travis Scott budapesti bulija
stories.summary: Van, akinek minden pénzt megért, más vegyes érzésekkel távozott Travis Scott káoszos csepeli bulijáról. A helyszínen a fanatikus rajongók mellett T. Danny, Manuel és a helikopterrel bereptetett Beton.Hofi is nyilatkozott a Telexnek.

stories.title: Orbán Anita először a holland külügyminisztert fogadta hivatalos látogatáson
stories.summary: A legfontosabb téma az Erasmus-ösztöndíjak voltak.

stories.title: Júliusban érkezik Charli XCX új albuma, aminek a borítóján Martin Scorsese is befigyel
stories.summary: A legendás filmrendező mellett John Cale zeneszerző és Marc Jacobs divattervező szerepelnek a lemezborítón.

stories.title: A kétharmados többség bármit megtehet – kreatív jogászkodással távolíthatják el a köztársasági elnököt
stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás nem mondott le a határidőig, és a miniszterelnök bejelentette: Alaptörvény-módosítással mozdítják el a pozíciójából, de hogy hogyan, azt egyelőre a frakciójának se árulta el. Az Alaptörvény módosítása felett nincs tartalmi kontroll, így a tiszások szinte bármit beleírhatnak a szövegbe.

stories.summary: Az új budapesti elnök Szatmáry Kristóf lesz, aki a választáson 30 százalékponttal kapott ki a választókerületében.

stories.summary: Az új kormány célja a profiltisztítás, kiveszik a gyógyszerfelügyeletet a népegészségügyi központból.

stories.title: Olyan rossz a magyar intézmények állapota, hogy ez most már komoly növekedési tartalék
stories.summary: A magyar gazdaság problémáit általában az inflációval, a gyenge növekedéssel vagy az államháztartási hiánnyal szoktuk magyarázni. Pedig van egy ennél mélyebb és hosszabb távon súlyosabb folyamat is: az ország intézményi környezetének romlása.

stories.title: Magyar Péter váratlan ajándéka, egymásra mutogatás és a katázás ígérete – ez történt a parlament hétfői ülésén
stories.summary: Magyar Péter napirend előtt összesen több mint egy órán át beszélt, újra hosszan kiosztotta az ellenzéki pártokat, és váratlan ajándékot adott Toroczkai Lászlónak. Az interpellációk alatt parázs viták alakultak ki, Hegedűs Zsolt vastapsot kapott. Közvetítésünk a parlament hétfői üléséről.

stories.title: Kövesdi Veronika: Sulyok céltáblává tudott válni, jelenléte összesűríti a még itt lévő rendszermaradványokat
stories.summary: A médiakutató arról is beszélt a Telex 2026 című műsorában, hogy Magyar Péternek két perszónája is párhuzamosan épül: a vitázó-gúnyos és a szakértői.

stories.title: A koncert előtt rosszul lett a zenekar billentyűse, a közönségből ugrottak be zongorázni
stories.summary: Az egyik dal legbonyolultabb részénél végül improvizált a fiatal beugrós, de így is hatalmas sikert aratott.

stories.title: What Hungary must do to receive EU funds frozen under Orban
stories.summary: Prime Minister Peter Magyar and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a deal that could free up over €16 billion in EU funds for Hungary. Now, Magyar's new government needs to deliver.

stories.title: 'I will sleep with fear': Romanians shaken after block of flats hit by drone
stories.summary: A Russian drone hit a block of flats in Romania on Friday, causing a fire and injuring two people, Romanian officials say.

stories.title: Romania: First footage shows damage after drone hits residential block near Ukraine border
stories.summary: A Russian drone from strikes on Ukraine crashes in Galati, injuring two and prompting Romania to seek faster NATO anti-drone support for its air defences.

stories.title: Euronews on the scene after Russian drone strike in Romania
stories.summary: Euronews reports from the Romanian city of Galati, where a Russian drone struck an apartment building near the Ukrainian border. The impact sparked a fire, injured two people and prompted an overnight emergency response.

stories.summary: Nato has vowed to ‘defend every inch’ of its territory after a Russian drone hit an apartment building in Romania. The strike prompted swift condemnation and threats of repercussion from European leaders. So is there a risk the war could expand beyond Ukraine? Lucy Hough speaks to senior international correspondent Peter Beaumont Continue reading...

stories.title: Moment Russian drone hits apartment block in Romania
stories.summary: Video captured the moment a Russian Geran-2 drone crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania near the Ukrainian border. The impact sparked a fire and injured two people, according to Romanian authorities.

stories.summary: Mass Russian drone strike targets Ukraine as 232 drones launched, 217 downed; Odesa and Zaporizhzhia suffer damage and injuries, ships hit in Black Sea.

stories.title: EU unblocks €16 billion in Hungary assistance as Magyar promises reforms
stories.summary: Reforms under new Prime Minister Peter Magyar will enable the EU to free up billions in frozen assistance for Budapest. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen announced the news as Magyar visited Brussels.

stories.title: Romania's political crisis deepens
stories.summary: After Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan’s government fell in early May, Romania's moderate parties have struggled to chart a new course — opening the door for the far right to gain ground.

stories.title: Several protesters arrested at New Jersey ICE facility as clashes continue
stories.summary: Dozens reportedly arrested for defying curfew at Delaney Hall in Newark amid hunger and labor strikes at facility On Monday afternoon, over an hour south of Newark, a few dozen protesters outside the New Jersey state legislature in Trenton condemned Democratic governor Mikie Sherrill’s decision to send in the state police to Delaney Hall, the Newark immigration detention center that has seen more than a week of chaotic and often violent clashes. stories.summary: Democrats decry president’s ‘most brazen act of self-dealing yet’ and vow to challenge fund in Congress Donald Trump is reconsidering whether to keep pressing for a $1.8bn fund to compensate his allies, a person familiar with his thinking said on Monday, as the justice department paused the program to comply with a court order. Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund has faced legal setbacks since it was announced two weeks ago. The idea has also faced a mounting political backlash from Republicans co

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Israel says rockets fired at country’s north after Trump announced shooting would ‘stop’
stories.summary: The US president said Benjamin Netanyahu had promised not to send troops to Beirut, while Hezbollah had agreed that ‘all shooting will stop’ The exchange of strikes between the US and Iran reflects the fragility of the current ceasefire, which has seen repeated violations even as American and Iranian officials try to negotiate a deal to extend it. Iran has maintained its chokehold on the strait of Hormuz, disrupting global energy supplies as a fifth of all oil and natural gas traded once passed

stories.title: Albanian authorities probe seaside resort project linked to Jared Kushner
stories.summary: Albanian anti-corruption prosecutors are investigating a luxury resort project linked to U.S. President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner, as protests from citizens and environmental organizations over the development persist. The project includes the uninhabited Adriatic island of Sazan and several hundred hectares of the Vjosa-Narta protected landscape, a sensitive coastal wetland area home to flamingos, seals and sea turtle nesting sites. stories.title: EU negotiators agree new migrant return law
stories.summary: Negotiators on Monday agreed new rules to speed up and increase deportations from the EU, including making it possible to send failed asylum-seekers to hubs outside the bloc. The text is part of sweeping reforms the EU is rolling out to increase control over who crosses its external borders and to support countries that receive the most external migrants, with Monday’s agreement landing just days before other migration and asylum reforms start applying on June 12. stories.title: Mexico City police teargas teachers’ protest 10 days before World Cup
stories.summary: Teachers associated with CNTE union were marching toward Zócalo for salary raises and reversal of pension laws Riot police fired teargas at teachers who were marching toward Mexico City’s historic Zócalo plaza, just days before the square is expected to host the 2026 World Cup “Fan Fest”. The incident is the second time police have clashed with teachers in the past week, and more conflict is likely as Mexico City prepares to hold the opening game of the Fifa World Cup on 11 June. “This event wil

stories.title: UK’s growing green economy worth more than £100bn a year, research finds
stories.summary: Net zero industry accounts for more than a million jobs and benefits whole country, according to CBI Economics More than a million jobs, higher wages, nearly half a trillion pounds in investment in the pipeline – the UK’s green economy is powering ahead, according to research by the country’s leading business organisation. The net zero economy, which is worth more than £100bn a year, benefits all of the UK, according to the CBI Economics analysis commissioned by the Energy and Climate Intelligen

stories.title: About 3 million workers on minimum wage to receive 4.75% pay rise in Fair Work Commission ruling
stories.summary: Unions had demanded 6% pay increase for lowest paid after war in Middle East pushed inflation higher Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Nearly 3 million workers will receive a 4.75% pay rise, while about 100,000 of the country’s lowest paid will receive a higher 6% increase, after the Fair Work Commission handed down its annual minimum wage decision. stories.title: UK wins court case over canceled plan to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
stories.summary: An international court has rejected a Rwandan claim for millions in damages over a scrapped deal to send asylum seekers to the East African nation.

stories.title: UN urges calm as Israel advances into Lebanon and Iran threatens counter-fronts
stories.summary: The UN has called on all sides to respect the ceasefire following an Israeli military push into Lebanon, which threatens to derail US-Iran peace talks. Israel ordered strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs as Hezbollah targeted Haifa, prompting Iran to threaten the opening of new fronts.

stories.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
stories.summary: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled. The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Top UK minister’s Mandelson messages missing after second reported phone theft
stories.summary: LONDON — Messages between Peter Mandelson and a second high-ranking British government figure are missing from the public disclosures forced by the Jeffrey Epstein scandal because of another reported phone theft, according to documents seen by POLITICO. stories.summary: Tehran says it holds Israel and US responsible, while Trump says ‘going silent’ on negotiations ‘would be very good’ Middle East live – latest updates Iran has indicated it will suspend peace talks with the US in protest against Israel’s offensive in Lebanon, threatening the collapse of negotiations with Washington as the two sides skirmished amid a faltering ceasefire. The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said: “The ceasefire between Iran and the US is unequivocally a ceasefire on all

stories.title: Trump says Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to ‘stop all shooting’
stories.summary: Declaration comes after Iran had pulled out of peace talks in protest at Israeli offensive in Lebanon Donald Trump has said Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to mutual de-escalation and to scale back fighting, seemingly averting an Israeli strike on Beirut and the potential collapse of ceasefire talks with Iran. The US president said in a social media post that he spoke to Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and representatives of Hezbollah and both agreed that “all shooting will stop”.

stories.title: Reform UK support could plateau as it relies on socially conservative views, study finds
stories.summary: Party could struggle to push ratings as strategy increasingly focuses on views held by minority of voters, research finds Reform UK is becoming increasingly reliant on socially conservative views for political support, and therefore could struggle to push its poll ratings much higher, a large-scale research project led by the leading psephologist John Curtice has found. A study of Nigel Farage’s party carried out as part of the British Social Attitudes report found that while Reform supporters w

stories.summary: Revocation of access newest attempt by Trump’s defense department to restrict reporting on military affairs Journalists may no longer enter the Pentagon’s press office, which has been designated as a classified space amid growing moves to restrict press access to the defense department. “This is the most transparent war department in history. stories.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
stories.summary: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

stories.summary: Bodycam video from Florida shows a police officer issuing a texting-while-driving citation to a woman, despite her not having a right hand. The ticket has since been recommended for dismissal, according to US media reports.

stories.summary: A British helicopter provided support during the operation on Sunday, the UK ministry of defence says.

stories.title: France bans Israel from major weapons show
stories.summary: PARIS — France has banned Israel from participating in Eurosatory, one of the world’s largest arms shows that kicks off later this month, the Israeli government announced on Monday. “The French decision encompasses: a ban on government representatives attending the exhibition; a ban on opening an Israeli national pavilion; and a restriction limiting Israeli defense industries to displaying air defense products only, with offensive systems explicitly excluded,” the Israeli

stories.title: ‘A shock to all Lebanese’: Israel sends a message as it takes ancient fort
stories.summary: Conquering of Beaufort Castle for first time in 26 years brings back memories of occupation of south Middle East crisis – live updates When Hussain Alawieh used to take tourists to Beaufort Castle, they would marvel at the view. The ancient hilltop fort, captured nearly 1,000 years earlier by Crusaders, still offered the same sweeping panoramic views of south Lebanon and the Litani River that empires fought over for a millennia. stories.title: EasyJet is an obvious takeover target, but US approach may not be a flyer | Nils Pratley
stories.summary: Castlelake’s move raises questions over valuation and ownership rules as well as whether Stelios Haji-Ioannou could throw a spanner in the works EasyJet says US takeover bid would be ‘highly opportunistic’ A share price gain of only 10% on a possible takeover approach is a meek reaction. If the stock market truly believed that Castlelake, a US investment fund, stood a decent chance of buying easyJet , you would expect the target’s stock to fly significantly higher. stories.title: Explosion at Malta fireworks factory sends plumes of smoke into air – video
stories.summary: The explosion happened at the Ta' Lourdes factory on the north side of the Mediterranean island on Monday morning. Two men in fields near the factory at the time of the blast were admitted to hospital with minor injuries. Residents in the surrounding area reported shattered windows and buildings shaken by the force of the blast. It is not yet known what caused the explosion Continue reading...

stories.title: Mandelson received sensitive Foreign Office briefings before vetting finished

stories.title: Sadiq Khan vows to overrule residents’ group’s objections to Soho bars and restaurants
stories.summary: London mayor says Soho Society’s decision to challenge all new licensing applications is ‘bad’ for city Sadiq Khan, the London mayor, has suggested he will overrule a residents’ society that has vowed to challenge all new applications for pubs and restaurants in Soho. stories.title: Florida lawsuit accuses OpenAI of ignoring safety warnings and putting children at risk
stories.summary: State sues maker of ChatGPT and CEO Sam Altman, alleging company ‘allowed a dangerous product to reach millions’ Florida filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, and its CEO, Sam Altman, on Monday alleging that the company concealed serious safety risks with its chatbot. Florida is the first state in the US to sue the artificial intelligence company. stories.title: White Castle adds veggie burger to menu as plant-based eating grows
stories.summary: Ohio-based slider chain introduces Southwest Veggie Slider permanently after customer feedback for meatless option Vegetarians have a new reason to celebrate: White Castle has added a permanent meat-free option to its menu. stories.summary: Marine Rescue NSW volunteer coaxes distressed dog on to jetski after it paddled to Snapper Island in Batemans Bay Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast On Monday, radio operators in Batemans Bay along the New South Wales south coast got a call they hadn’t had before: a dog had been swept off the rocks and was in the ocean in distress. stories.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
stories.summary: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeks A far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting. stories.title: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic
stories.summary: President Macron says ship subject to sanctions and posts video of operation that took place with UK support A suspected Russian oil tanker has been detained in the Atlantic, France has announced, in the latest seizure aimed at combatting Moscow’s “shadow fleet” of vessels contravening international sanctions. The Tagor was detained on Sunday morning in international waters more than 400 nautical miles (740km) west of Brittany with the help of the UK and other partners, said the French president

stories.title: French navy boards Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic – video
stories.summary: France has released footage that appears to show French naval commandos boarding a ship suspected of being part of Russia's 'shadow fleet' and subject to international sanctions. The French president, Emmanuel Macron, said the operation took place on the tanker, named the Tagor, with support of the UK and in strict compliance with the law of the sea. stories.title: Baden-Württemberg restricts smoking in public spaces
stories.summary: The southwestern German state of Baden-Württemberg has expanded restrictions on smoking in public spaces like outdoor swimming pools, theme parks and zoos.

stories.title: Wildfires devastating richer areas but fewer hectares burned globally – study

Catastrophic blazes claimed lives, homes and jobs last year in California, Canada, Europe and South Korea. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea. stories.title: Távozik a CÖF éléről Csizmadia László, ifj. Lomnici Zoltán veszi át a helyét
stories.summary: Csizmadia László a lemondását azzal indokolta, hogy át kell adnia a kuratórium vezetését azoknak a fiataloknak, akik az elmúlt években mögé felsorakoztak.

stories.title: WSJ: Felesége szerint szexuális tartalmú üzeneteket küldözgetett nőknek a maine-i demokrata szenátorjelölt
stories.summary: Graham Platner azóta törölt Reddit-fiókján olyan hozzászólásokat is találtak, amelyek bagatellizálták a szexuális erőszakot.

stories.title: Felmondott a sokat kritizált, egyedi gyógyszerkérelmeket elbíráló Batthyány-Strattmann alapítvány szakmai igazgatója
stories.summary: Pozsgay Csilla eddig félállásban töltötte be a tisztséget, távozását azzal indokolta, a kérelmek nagy száma miatt főállású szakmai igazgatóra lenne szükség, amit ő nem tud vállalni.

stories.title: Fóliázva
Megduplázta a nyereségét a Hunguard, Rogán Antal feltalálótársai a teljes egymilliárdos profitot kivennék
stories.summary: Árbevétel és profit terén is rekordévet zárt a gyakran jókor jó helyen lévő IT-biztonsági cég.

stories.title: Arte: How do we save a museum sector in crisis?
stories.summary: Many are increasingly relying on lucrative corporate sponsorship deals – but that has sparked criticism.

stories.title: Arte: Segítség híján problémás helyekről keríthetnek pénzt a válságban lévő európai múzeumok
stories.summary: Egyre több múzeum próbál jelentős vállalati szponzorációkra támaszkodni, ez a megoldást viszont sokat kritizálták.

stories.title: Több mint ezer Marilyn Monroe-hasonmás döntött Guinness-rekordot
stories.summary: A 20. század legnagyobb szexszimbóluma 100 éve született, erre az alkalomra szervezték a rekorddöntést.

stories.title: Újra kihajózhatott a hantavírus miatt hírhedtté vált Hondius luxus-óceánjáró
stories.summary: Májusban kisebb pánikot okozott a hantavírus megjelenése a hajón, sokan a Covid korai szakaszához hasonlították a helyzetet – alaptalanul.

stories.title: Krimi is meg nem is, traumaregény is meg nem is
stories.summary: Pethő Balázs mintha menet közben próbálna meg lavírozni a műfajok között, ami összeütközhet egyes olvasók elvárásaival. Kritika.

stories.title: Goodbye, Norma Jeane: How Marilyn Monroe changed Hollywood
stories.summary: Long seen primarily as a sex symbol, Marilyn Monroe is now viewed as an early advocate for women in Hollywood. On the 100th anniversary of her birth, DW looks at the star who balanced fame and feminist agency.

stories.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
stories.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

stories.title: How the Alevi community came to thrive in Germany
stories.summary: The Alevis make up the fourth largest religious community in Germany today. What are the main tenets of their faith? And how are they preserving their unique culture?

stories.title: Germany news: Most expect AfD state premier after elections
stories.summary: Most people in Germany expect a premier from the far-right AfD to lead at least one state after elections in the autumn, a survey has found. The Brenner Pass linking Austria and Italy has reopened.

stories.title: Pashinyan on course for landslide victory and pro-West mandate in Armenia election, new poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll projects Armenian Premier Nikol Pashinyan’s party could win a large parliamentary majority, as Russia intensifies its campaign to counter Yerevan's pro-West realignment, warning of a Ukraine scenario.

stories.title: French police detain hundreds in Paris after violent celebrations of PSG’s Champions League title
stories.summary: The French interior ministry said that some 780 persons were questioned nationally, including 283 in Paris alone.

stories.title: Israel retakes Beaufort castle in Lebanon, Paris issues strong condemnation
stories.summary: Israeli forces push deeper into Lebanon and raise their flag over Beaufort Castle, a strategic fortress. France condemns the move and demands a UN Security Council meeting.

stories.title: Laos cave survivors help with plan to find last two missing men
stories.summary: So far, five men have been freed from the narrow, flooded cave but efforts are ongoing to locate two more.

stories.title: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
stories.summary: After a day of uncertainty, the government confirms the team will depart for Mexico on Monday.

stories.title: Dead whale towed ashore in Denmark ahead of autopsy
stories.summary: "Timmy" was found dead off the coast of Anholt despite multiple attempts to rescue it.

stories.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
stories.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.

stories.title: Nicaraguan indigenous leader dies after three years in prison
stories.summary: Rights groups condemn the death of Brooklyn Rivera, 73, following his long arbitrary detention by the governing regime.

stories.title: Trump seeking edits to US-Iran deal, US media report
stories.summary: The requested changes are related to the Strait of Hormuz and the removal of highly enriched uranium, according to US media.

stories.title: Caribbean hot sauce producers warn of shortages and higher prices
stories.summary: Manufacturers in Jamaica say the key chilli peppers they need are in limited supply.

stories.title: A year of grief after Air India crash: What remains when a plane falls from the sky
stories.summary: A mother still speaks about her son who died in the crash in the present tense and a brother waits for answers.

stories.title: Grammy-winning director explores his Nigerian grandfather's role in the Biafran war
stories.summary: Filmmaker Meji Alabi directs a landmark BBC Africa Eye documentary about Nigeria's civil war.

stories.title: Afghan refugee truck crash kills 18, injures 35 in eastern Afghanistan
stories.summary: Zabihullah Mujahid, the spokesperson for the Taliban government, expressed deep sorrow over the tragedy, which took place at the conclusion of the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Adha, and offered condolences to the families of the victims

stories.title: UK, US and Australia to develop 'cutting-edge' underwater drone technology
stories.summary: The new technology is expected to be ready by 2027 and is designed to help defend critical subsea cables and pipelines.

stories.title: Trump's doctor says he's in excellent health - but are US presidents' health checks a PR exercise?
stories.summary: Every president in modern history - including Donald Trump this week - has taken an annual physical exam that is as much about messaging as it is about health.

stories.title: Eighteen people killed in Afghanistan truck crash, including 10 children
stories.summary: Truck was carrying Afghan families returning Pakistan when it overturned, official says A truck overturned in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing 18 people on board including 10 children, a provincial official told Agence France-Presse. Deadly traffic crashes are common in Afghanistan, due in part to poor roads after decades of conflict, dangerous driving and a lack of regulation. Continue reading...
